source: Invest Like the Best (YouTube) — GPUs, TPUs, economics of AI (Nvidia vs Google)
summary: OpenAI is a high-cost token producer due to paying margins to third-party compute providers, creating a structural disadvantage
summary_extended: Baker argues that OpenAI is structurally disadvantaged as a high-cost token producer because it relies on third-party compute providers (primarily Microsoft Azure) and pays margins to those providers, who may not be optimal GPU operators. This cost structure contrasts sharply with competitors like xAI, which owns its own infrastructure, and Google, which uses proprietary TPUs — both of which have lower per-token production costs. Baker uses this structural disadvantage to explain OpenAI's aggressive $1.4 trillion Stargate spending commitment and the reported 'code red' urgency within the organization, framing these as reactive moves driven by economic necessity rather than strategic confidence. Critically, Baker argues OpenAI will not be able to close this per-token cost gap before xAI and Google gain additional advantages from Blackwell and GB300 deployments, suggesting OpenAI's competitive position may deteriorate further in the near term. The investment implication is that Google (as a vertically integrated TPU operator) is structurally advantaged in the frontier model race, while Microsoft's position as OpenAI's compute provider may be more complicated than it appears — collecting margins but hosting a structurally weakened partner.
detail: OpenAI pays margins to compute providers who may not be optimal GPU operators, making them a high-cost token producer relative to XAI (owns infrastructure) and Google (TPUs). This explains the $1.4T Stargate spending commitment and 'code red' urgency. They will not fix this per-token cost disadvantage before XAI and Google have Blackwell/GB300 advantages.
confidence_evidence: Baker frames this as explanatory of OpenAI's behavior but acknowledges uncertainty about their internal economics
quote_fragment: OpenAI is a high-cost producer of tokens

ticker_audit.reasoning: Microsoft is OpenAI's primary compute provider and the entity collecting the margins that make OpenAI a high-cost token producer. The thesis is partly about this relationship and its implications for OpenAI's competitive position, making MSFT a direct subject of the structural dynamic described.

Baker explicitly names Google as a structural beneficiary of this thesis — Google's TPU-based vertical integration gives it lower per-token costs than OpenAI, making it a direct beneficiary of OpenAI's cost disadvantage in the frontier model competition.
